title: Query & Path Parameters
overview: Pass query parameters and path parameters dynamically using REST Assured methods.
whyItMatters: Endpoints dynamically load data based on identifiers (Path Parameters) or filters (Query Parameters). Automating tests for these endpoints requires dynamic parameter passing.
explanation: Query parameters are appended to the URL after a question mark (e.g., `?page=2&sort=desc`). In REST Assured, use `queryParam("key", "value")`. Path parameters are part of the URL path itself, like `/users/42`, defined via placeholders like `/users/{id}`. You populate them using `pathParam("id", 42)`. You can also use `queryParams()` to pass multiple query parameters as a Map.
keyPoints: Use `.pathParam("placeholder", "value")` with `{placeholder}` in the endpoint string.
REST Assured handles URL encoding automatically for query parameters.
Pass multiple parameters using Maps with `.queryParams()` and `.pathParams()`.
codeExample:
given().
    baseUri("https://api.example.com").
    pathParam("userId", 123).
    queryParam("status", "active").
    queryParam("page", 1).
when().
    get("/users/{userId}/orders").
then().
    statusCode(200);
realWorldExample: When testing a search API, you pass the search term and filters as query parameters. When fetching a specific user's profile, you pass the user's ID as a path parameter.
commonMistakes: Confusing query parameters with path parameters.
Forgetting to include the `{}` placeholder in the URL string when using `pathParam`, causing an exception.
Manually URL-encoding values before passing them to `queryParam`, which can result in double-encoding.
bestPractices: Avoid hardcoding IDs in URLs; use `pathParam` to make tests readable and flexible.
Use Maps for endpoints that require many query parameters to keep the given() block clean.
Rely on REST Assured's automatic URL encoding.

interviewQuestions.q: What is the difference between a query parameter and a path parameter?
interviewQuestions.a: A path parameter is part of the URI structure used to identify a specific resource (e.g., `/users/123`). A query parameter is appended to the URI to sort, filter, or paginate the resource collection (e.g., `/users?status=active`).

interviewQuestions.q: How do you pass a path parameter in REST Assured?
interviewQuestions.a: You use `.pathParam("paramName", value)` in the `given()` block and reference it as `{paramName}` in the URI string in the `when()` block.

revisionSummary: Use queryParam() for ?key=value filters and pathParam() for /resource/{id} routing.
